title: Checkbox Assessments Aren't Fit to Measure to Risk
summary: Security governance needs to be more than an annual compliance exercise. New companies are emerging to address risk-management gaps in current audit tools.

title: Attackers Weaponize RubyGems for Data Dead Drops
summary: Threat actors are publishing RubyGems packages that include scrapers targeting public-facing UK government servers, but with no clear objective.

title: Tables Turn on 'The Gentlemen' RaaS Gang With Data Leak
summary: An OPSEC failure provides a window into what helped the ransomware group rise: a generous affiliate model, opportunistic TTPs, and an effective organizational structure.

title: Dark Reading Celebrates 20 Years as a Leading Authority on Cybersecurity, Highlighting the People, Events, Ideas, and Technologies Shaping the Modern Risk Landscape
summary: Informa TechTarget's flagship cybersecurity media brand launches a special content series to mark two decades as a trusted source for cybersecurity professionals.

title: China's 'FamousSparrow' APT Nests in South Caucasus Energy Firm
summary: The cyberthreat group targets an Azerbaijani oil and gas firm with repeated attacks, as the China-linked actors extend targeting beyond hospitality, telecom, and government sectors.

title: LatAm Vibe Hackers Generate Custom Hacking Tools on the Fly
summary: In the latest evolution of automated cyberattacks, two threat campaigns heavily leveraged AI agents to support attacks against entities in Mexico and Brazil.

title: It's Patch Tuesday for Microsoft & Not a Zero-Day In Sight
summary: It's the first time in two years with no zero-days. But with 137 flaws to patch, including nine critical ones, admins still have plenty of work to do.

title: Hugging Face Packages Weaponized With a Single File Tweak
summary: A tokenizer library file present in Hugging Face AI models can be manipulated to hijack the model's outputs and exfiltrate data.

title: 20 Leaders Who Built the CISO Era: 2 Decades of Change
summary: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary special coverage, we profile the CISOs, founders, researchers, criminals, and policymakers who rewrote the enterprise risk playbook.

title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
summary: Hundreds of npm packages infected by the self-propagating, credential-stealing worm from TeamPCP are related to the open source TanStack ecosystem.

title: FCC Softens Ban on Foreign-Made Routers
summary: The Federal Communications Commission eased some restrictions and pushed back deadlines for foreign router manufacturers, but the ban is still in place.

title: Tech Can't Stop These Threats — Your People Can
summary: Security controls can do only so much. Here are four attacks where your employees are usually your first, and only, line of cyber defense.

title: 'Dirty Frag' Exploit Poised to Blow Up on Enterprise Linux Distros
summary: The privilege escalation vulnerability, which is similar to other Linux flaws like Copy Fail and Dirty Pipe, may already be under limited exploitation.

title: Hackers Use AI for Exploit Development, Attack Automation
summary: Cyber adversaries have long used AI, but now attackers are using large language models to develop exploits and orchestrate complex attacks.

title: Cyber Espionage Group Targets Aviation Firms to Steal Map Data
summary: The campaign quietly compromises aerospace and drone operators to exfiltrate GIS files, terrain models, and GPS data and gain a clear picture of adversaries' world view.

title: ShinyHunters Claims Second Attack Against Instructure
summary: The edtech company is struggling to wrest control from its hackers. PII belonging to hundreds of millions of people is on the line.

title: After Replacing TeamPCP Malware, 'PCPJack' Steals Cloud Secrets
summary: PCPJack makes innovative use of parquet files for stealthy, pre-validated target discovery as it canvasses multiple cloud environments.

title: Has CISA Finally Found Its New Leader in Tom Parker?
summary: Dark Reading investigates rumors that Tom Parker, a board room "operator" and longtime cyber exec, could be next in line to take over CISA.

title: 'TrustFall' Convention Exposes Claude Code Execution Risk
summary: Malicious repositories can trigger code execution in Claude Code, Cursor CLI, Gemini CLI, and CoPilot CLI with minimal or no user interaction, thanks to skimpy warning dialogs.

title: AI-Driven Cyberattack on Mexico Couldn't Breach OT Systems
summary: The most sophisticated AI-integrated campaign to date hit a brick wall in the form of a SCADA login screen.

title: VoidStealer Malware Darts Past Google Chrome's Encryption
summary: Authors of the VoidStealer Trojan uncovered yet another way to get around Google's App-Bound Encryption (ABE), opening the door to infostealers.

title: Instructure Breach Exposes Schools' Vendor Dependence
summary: ShinyHunters' attack on Instructure, which owns the widely used Canvas learning management system (LMS), carries big questions about the trust educational institutions put into their vendors.

title: From Stuxnet to ChatGPT: 20 News Events That Shaped Cyber
summary: As part of its 20th anniversary celebration, Dark Reading looks back on 20 of the biggest newsmaking events from the past two decades that influenced the risk landscape for today's cybersecurity teams.

title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
summary: In hard-to-detect attacks, hackers are dropping the CloudZ RAT and a fresh plug-in, Pheno, to hijack the Windows-based bridge between PCs and smartphones.

title: Middle East Cyber Battle Field Broadens — Especially in UAE
summary: As the war with Iran continues, breach attempts targeting the United Arab Emirates tripled in a few weeks — many targeting critical infrastructure.

title: Trellix Source Code Breach Highlights Growing Supply Chain Threats
summary: Info is scant, but such breaches can reveal where a security product's controls are located and how detections are designed, giving attackers a leg up.

title: Research Hub Bridges Cybersecurity Gap for Under-Resourced Organizations
summary: The UC Berkeley Center for Long-Term Cybersecurity (CLTC) offers tools and support to schools, local governments, and non-profits as they defend themselves against a growing volume of cyberattacks.

title: Why Security Leadership Makes or Breaks a Pen Test
summary: Well-run security drills go beyond checking audit boxes to identifying and addressing trouble spots. Effective leaders ensure proper scope, access, and follow-through, but it's not easy.

title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
summary: A proof-of-concept exploit (PoC) shows how someone with admin privileges can exploit the issue to steal passwords, and thus use them to engage in further malicious activity.

title: How the Story of a USB Penetration Test Went Viral
summary: Two decades ago, pen tester Steve Stasiukonis caused a sensation by sprinkling rigged thumb drives around a credit union parking lot and following what curious employees did next. This episode looks back at the history-making event.

title: Physical Cargo Theft Gets a Boost From Cybercriminals
summary: Cargo theft is no longer about small groups of criminals operating on the ground, but transnational cybercriminal syndicates using access to supply chain systems to reroute goods.

title: RMM Tools Fuel Stealthy Phishing Campaign
summary: Attackers are abusing two remote monitoring and management (RMM) tools to evade detection in a campaign that has impacted over 80 organizations so far.

title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
summary: Shortly after the authentication-bypass flaw was disclosed multiple proof-of-concept exploits appeared, and one researcher claims there's been zero-day activity for at least a month.

title: Silver Fox Springs Tax-Themed Attacks on Orgs in India, Russia
summary: More than 1,600 socially engineered messages from the China-backed advanced persistent threat (APT) group target various sectors to deliver the previously undocumented ABCDoor backdoor, ValleyRAT, and other malware.

title: How Dark Reading Lifted Off the Launchpad in 2006
summary: Twenty years ago, this media brand didn't have a print edition to attract eyeballs and sponsors. Top-notch content and editorial talent did the heavy lifting.

title: 76% of All Crypto Stolen in 2026 Is Now in North Korea
summary: North Korean threat actors are pulling off historic cryptocurrency heists on a yearly, sometimes weekly basis now. AI might be helping them.

title: If AI's So Smart, Why Does It Keep Deleting Production Databases?
summary: The issue isn't artificial intelligence, but rather an industry adding AI agent integrations into production environments before proper security testing.

title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Security) Progress
summary: Feeling creative? Have something to say about the last 20 years of cybersecurity? Our editors will award the best cybersecurity-related caption with a $20 gift card.

title: 20 Years in Cyber: Dark Reading Marks Milestone With Month of Special Coverage
summary: On this day in 2006, Dark Reading went live. We have a celebration planned that spans our two decades of covering the industry, and you, dear readers, are invited.

title: TeamPCP Hits SAP Packages With 'Mini Shai-Hulud' Attack
summary: Several npm packages for SAP's cloud application development ecosystem have been compromised as TeamPCP's supply chain attacks broaden.

title: Another AI-Assisted Software Scan Yields 9-Year-Old Linux Bug
summary: The proof-of-concept exploit code runs only 10 lines long, but luckily, a patch is already available.

title: Anthropic's Mythos Has Landed: Here's What Comes Next for Cyber
summary: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how the new AI model threatens to completely upend cybersecurity, and what industry leaders are telling the press.

title: Oracle Red Bull Racing Team Revs Up Automation to Boost Security
summary: While drivers race to shave off seconds on the track, the team's IT and engineering staff are speeding up how they deliver security.

title: Claude Mythos Fears Startle Japan's Financial Services Sector
summary: Global financial institutions are panicked over Anthropic's new superhacker AI model. Cyber experts aren't quite as worried.

title: Reverse Engineering With AI Unearths High-Severity GitHub Bug
summary: Wiz used an AI reverse-engineering tool to pinpoint a vulnerability that previously would have been too costly and time-consuming to undertake.

title: AI Finds 38 Security Flaws in Electronic Health Record Platform
summary: Flaws in OpenEMR's platform — used by more than 100,000 healthcare providers — enabled database compromise, remote code execution, and data theft.

title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
summary: The emerging ransomware has been deployed against victims of the TeamPCP supply chain attacks, but organizations should think twice before paying for a decryptor.

title: Lotus Wiper Attack Targets Venezuelan Energy Firms, Utilities
summary: An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.

title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
summary: The North Korean group is using stolen victim videos, AI-generated avatars, and fake Zoom calls to scale malware attacks against cryptocurrency executives.

title: NSA Chief During Snowden Affair Shares Regrets, Reflections 13 Years Later
summary: Chris Inglis was the head civilian in charge at the NSA when the Snowden leaks exploded. He gets candid about mistakes the organization made, and what CISOs need to know about spotting potential threats, media disclosures, and "enculturation."
